title: Quiz 2 and Material Instances

video.note: Covers creating material instances from parent materials, exposing scalar/vector parameters, Apply/Save behavior, real-time material instance adjustments, normal flatness, roughness strength, color tint, resetting defaults, and efficient material variation. Includes Quiz 2 as the opening checkpoint.

learningTarget: I can explain core material concepts and create an editable material instance.

bellRinger.prompt: Why might a studio want many variations of one material?

vocabulary.definition: A child version of a material that can expose editable settings without rebuilding the whole material graph.
The original material that controls the structure and available parameters for its instances.
A material value exposed so it can be edited from a material instance.
A single-number parameter, often used for values such as size, roughness strength, or normal flatness.
A multi-value parameter often used for color tint or RGB values.
A texture slot exposed so a material instance can swap a texture map.
An enabled material instance value that replaces the parent material default.
A change that appears immediately in the viewport without recompiling the full material.
Returning an overridden material instance value back to the parent material value.
The material editor action that updates the parent material so new parameters appear in instances.

assignment.title: Material Instance Variation Set
evidenceRequired: Quiz 2 completion record or teacher checkoff.
Screenshot of the parent material showing at least two exposed parameters.
Screenshot of at least two material instance variations applied in the level.
Short reflection explaining how a material instance is different from a separate new material.

exitTicket: How is a material instance different from a separate new material?
